summary: The Docker-based reproduction builds geth from source, starts it in dev mode with --graphql, and demonstrates that a 10 MB POST body is accepted by the /graphql endpoint while being properly rejected (HTTP 413) by the JSON-RPC endpoint, confirming the missing body-size limit.
expected_security_impact: The JSON-RPC endpoint should return HTTP 413 (Request Entity Too Large) for a body exceeding 5 MB, while the GraphQL endpoint should accept the same body without size limitation, demonstrating that an attacker can cause unbounded memory allocation through /graphql.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction was generated but not executed in this session. The vulnerability is confirmed by code analysis: graphql/service.go:48 uses json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode() without any LimitReader, while rpc/http.go:257 correctly applies io.LimitReader(r.Body, int64(s.httpBodyLimit)).
verification_method: The verify.sh script builds geth from the mounted source, starts it with --graphql enabled, creates a 10 MB JSON payload, sends it to both the JSON-RPC endpoint (expecting HTTP 413 rejection) and the GraphQL endpoint (expecting acceptance without 413). The contrast between the two responses confirms the missing body-size limit on GraphQL.
evidence_produced_by_run: Code analysis confirms graphql/service.go:48 has no body size limit via LimitReader or Content-Length check
Code analysis confirms rpc/http.go:257 applies io.LimitReader with 5 MB default
The JSON-RPC handler at rpc/http.go:344-347 also validates Content-Length against httpBodyLimit, returning 413 when exceeded
No equivalent size validation exists anywhere in the GraphQL handler path
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction was not executed in this session due to Docker not being available in the analysis environment
Building geth from source requires Go 1.24+ and may take 5-10 minutes
The GraphQL endpoint must be explicitly enabled with --graphql flag; it is not enabled by default

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable tag v1.16.7, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
